items.title: Bourse de recherche sur la culture matérielle - Centre d’études francoprovençales « René Willien »
items.summary: Dans le cadre de sa mission de recherche sur le patrimoine alpin, le Centre d’études francoprovençales « René Willien » (Cefp) lance un appel à candidatures pour une bourse de recherche historique et ethnographique, d'une durée de quatre mois, sur les palettes à beurre de la tradition valdôtaine.

items.title: KADOC fellowship on Religion, Culture and Society 2026
items.summary: KADOC–KU Leuven annually awards one fellowship of up to three months to a young international scholar (M/F/X, with a maximum of ten years of academic seniority after obtaining a PhD) working on topics related to its main research fields. items.title: French Institutes for Advanced Study Fellowship Programme 2027/2028
items.summary: The FIAS Fellowhip Programme is open to all disciplines in the Social Sciences and the Humanities (SSH) and to all other research fields interfacing with the SSH. Applicants are strongly encouraged to consult the webpages of the host Institutes for Advanced Study (IAS) to learn more about their specific scientific priorities, areas of focus, and institutional partners, as these should be carefully

items.title: International Excellence in the Humanities Programme - Junior and Senior Research Fellowships 2027
items.summary: The International Excellence in the Humanities Fellowship Programme promotes academic diversity and interdisciplinarity and welcomes researchers with an international background to apply for post- doctoral fellowships at the MaCI. items.title: Assistant / Associate Professor (tenure track) or Professor in French
items.summary: The University of Turku, Faculty of Humanities, invites applications for the position of Assistant / Associate Professor (tenure track) or Professor in French. The employment will begin 1.8.2027. The position is located at the School of Languages and Translation Studies at the University of Turku, within the Department of French. items.title: Première conférence de la communauté de recherche en archéologie biomoléculaire et isotopique
items.summary: Depuis une dizaine d’années, le domaine des sciences archéologiques biomoléculaires connaît un essor remarquable, se traduisant par une diversification des approches (ADN ancien, biogéochimie isotopique, résidus organiques, paléoprotéomique, paléométabolomique, spectroscopie infra-rouge) et une multiplication des champs d’application — humains, animaux, végétaux, pathogènes ou encore sédiments.
